metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale (in What or a new Risk section) for why field evidence suffices to supersede the discriminates_novelty eval, OR add an AC for a post-rollout false-drop monitoring signal (e.g., compare dropped-finding re-emergence rate in the next N runs) to establish a circuit-breaker if scorer miscalibration is detected.
findings.finding: The plan states the operator has authorized superseding the discriminates_novelty-eval prerequisite on field evidence, but provides no positive rationale for why field evidence (demonstrating the convergence problem) substitutes for the discriminator-quality eval (demonstrating the novelty scorer correctly classifies findings). Activating novelty_drop_active=True by default without discriminator validation means a poorly-calibrated novelty scorer could silently drop real findings — the field evidence motivates urgency but does not validate scorer fitness.
findings.location: Why section / What section (shared gate explanation) / AC (no AC covers discriminator fitness)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:86` (section comment `# ── the triple gate (inert by default; evidence gate) ──`) to the plan's known-hits enumeration and specify how to update it (e.g., rename to 'active by default; explicit-False is the back-out').
findings.finding: The section comment at `tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:86` reads `# ── the triple gate (inert by default; evidence gate) ──` — a grep-catchable stale-framing hit in tests/ that matches `inert.*default`, but the plan's known-hits enumeration for that file only cites line :166 (the assertion). This is a residual stale comment the normative grep will surface that the enumeration silently omits.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the known-hits list: `tests/unit/test_plan_review_rising_floor.py:86` section comment — rename to `# ── the triple gate (active by default; explicit-False is the back-out) ──`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion requiring at least one end-to-end (or integration-level against a live/stubbed-but-realistic LLM boundary) smoke test — e.g. 'run a fixture ticket through `rebar review --plan` with default config and assert the plan-review rising floor activates when round ≥ 2' — to prove the newly-default paths exercise correctly post-flip.
findings.finding: The plan flips two flags to default-ON — activating the plan-review rising floor and the code-review region-gated floor for ALL production runs — but no acceptance criterion exercises either newly-default path end-to-end against the live system (live LLM / real ticket). AC3 and AC4 only verify the config gate activates (unit/offline), and `make test` is a suite-level command that can pass entirely on mocks. Per E6(f), a default-flip cutover requires at least one criterion that exercises the changed production path live, because green offline tests that bypass the LLM boundary don't prove the floor triggers correctly under real conditions.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('A test constructs a default VerifyConfig()...'), AC4 ('A test covers the cross-gate side-effect...'), and the `make test` AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A smoke/integration test (or a manual verification step with log evidence) confirms that with default VerifyConfig(), a multi-round plan-review session triggers `_maybe_apply_rising_floor` (plan-review) and that code-review workflow proceeds through the `novelty_drop_active` branch in workflow_ops.py:608, using a minimal fixture that does not bypass the gate dispatch path.' If a full live-LLM test is infeasible in CI, document the rationale and name the manual verification owner.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit deliverable entries to the What section describing the two new tests required by AC3 and AC4 — naming the target test file(s), what each test asserts, and whether they extend an existing module or are new.
findings.finding: AC3 and AC4 require new test code that is never described as a deliverable in the What section. What describes only two line changes and a doc/comment sweep of existing files; it never says 'write a new test that constructs a default VerifyConfig() with explicit-False variants' (AC3) or 'write a new test covering the code-review cross-gate side-effect' (AC4). These are orphan criteria with no corresponding described deliverable.
findings.location: What section (deliverables list) vs AC3 and AC4
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a 'New/extended tests' bullet: 'In tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py (or a shared config-defaults test module), add test_config_defaults_on_explicit_false_override: construct VerifyConfig() (assert remediation_mode is True, novelty_drop_active is True), then construct VerifyConfig(remediation_mode=False, novelty_drop_active=False) and assert the floor config-gate short-circuits. In tests/unit/test_code_review_region_floor.py (or workflow_ops test module), add test_cross_gate_default_config_proceeds: construct a default VerifyConfig() and assert the evidence-gate check at workflow_ops.py:608 does not short-circuit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out section (or a dedicated AC) stating that operators can restore prior behavior by setting `remediation_mode=False` and `novelty_drop_active=False` in their VerifyConfig, and note that this option will be removed when task 49ae lands.
findings.finding: The back-out/rollback mechanism for this default-behavior change is mentioned only in passing within a doc-sweep instruction ('naming explicit `false` as the back-out'), not as an explicit operator-facing rollback plan. T4 requires the remedy to be an explicit rollback/back-out plan — acknowledgement alone is insufficient.
findings.location: What section, RULE-BASED STALE-FRAMING SWEEP clause; no dedicated rollback section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Rollback' subsection in What: 'To revert to prior behavior set remediation_mode=False and novelty_drop_active=False in VerifyConfig (or the deployment config). This escape hatch will be removed when task 49ae-258b-4534-4d83 ships; any regression should be diagnosed before that task lands.' Add a corresponding AC: 'docs/plan-review-gate.md (or an operator runbook) documents the False-override rollback path and its removal timeline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or cite an AC that exercises the code-review region-gated floor's behavioral output (e.g., that a low-novelty submission's verdict is raised, or score threshold is applied) under a default VerifyConfig(), not just that the gate branch is entered — or explicitly cite the existing test(s) in test_plan_review_rising_floor.py / the four floor/gate modules that already provide this behavioral coverage with novelty_drop_active=True.
findings.finding: AC4 validates only that the code-review region-gated floor's evidence-gate check 'proceeds rather than short-circuiting' (i.e., gate entry), but does not require a test that the floor computation itself produces correct behavioral output (e.g., a verdict change or score comparison) under the newly-defaulted config. This is a cutover that makes the code-review floor the default for all users; if the floor logic contains a bug that manifests only when invoked by default config, AC4 goes green (gate entered) while the live path is broken. The plan does not explicitly cite pre-existing tests that exercise the code-review floor's behavioral output with novelty_drop_active=True, so there is no documented rationale that the offline-gate test is a faithful substitute.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC4 to: 'A test constructs a default VerifyConfig() and, using a fixture that simulates a low-novelty code-review submission, asserts the region-gated floor applies (e.g., returned WorkflowResult.verdict reflects the floor raise), OR cite the existing behavioral test in [named module] that already covers this with novelty_drop_active=True and confirm it runs under default config.' Alternatively, add a one-line note: 'Behavioral coverage of the floor computation under novelty_drop_active=True is already provided by [test name] in tests/unit/test_code_review_novelty_floor.py (added in ADR 0037 implementation); AC4 adds only the config-default regression guard.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:194` ('By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert') to the known-hits list in the What section's rule-based sweep enumeration.
findings.finding: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py line ~194 contains 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert' inside the _maybe_apply_rising_floor docstring — a stale-framing hit directly about novelty_drop_active that is NOT in the plan's known-hits list (which only enumerates :81 and :~523). The grep sweep will catch it via the 'default.*inert' or 'flag is False' pattern, but the plan's informative enumeration omits it, risking the implementer missing it without running the grep.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:194 (What section — RULE-BASED STALE-FRAMING SWEEP known-hits list)
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section's known-hits list, add: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:194` (`_maybe_apply_rising_floor` docstring: 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert and the verdict is byte-identical to a normal review').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:710` to the known-hits enumeration in the What section's rule-based stale-framing sweep.
findings.finding: src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:710 contains 'Gated on verify.novelty_drop_active + self-gates inert with no prior memory' — a stale-framing comment about novelty_drop_active that is NOT in the plan's known-hits list (which only names workflow_ops.py:597/608 for code-review stale hits). The plan's rule-based grep will fire on 'inert.*default' or similar patterns, but the informative enumeration omits it.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:710 (What section — RULE-BASED STALE-FRAMING SWEEP known-hits list)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow the grep pattern in AC #8 (and the What section sweep definition) to scope `flag is False` hits to only `remediation_mode` and `novelty_drop_active`, e.g. by piping through `grep -E 'remediation_mode|novelty_drop_active'`, so intentionally-kept-False flags (completion_floor_active) do not cause false failures.
findings.finding: The stale-framing grep sweep AC (AC #8) requires zero hits for the pattern `flag is False`, but `tests/unit/test_completion_floor.py:261` contains `assert vc.completion_floor_active is False` — a deliberately-kept False default (completion_floor_active is explicitly NON-GOALS in this plan). This will cause the sweep to return a hit and the AC to fail unless the grep is narrowed to the two specific flags or the pattern is scoped to exclude completion_floor_active.
findings.location: What section — RULE-BASED STALE-FRAMING SWEEP grep command definition; AC #8
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC #8: after the grep, pipe to `grep -E 'remediation_mode|novelty_drop_active'` — or change the pattern from `flag is False` to the two specific flag names — so completion_floor_active's kept-False assertion does not count as a stale hit.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Narrow the sweep AC's zero-hit criterion to explicitly exempt `completion_floor_active` (e.g., add 'excluding lines about `completion_floor_active`, which stays False by design'), or refine the grep pattern to match only `remediation_mode` and `novelty_drop_active` stale-framing hits.
findings.finding: The AC's normative sweep grep includes `'flag is False'` as a pattern, and `tests/unit/test_completion_floor.py:261` contains exactly `assert vc.completion_floor_active is False  # inert by default (the evidence gate)`. Since the NON-GOALS section explicitly keeps `completion_floor_active` at False, this line cannot be updated to satisfy a zero-hit requirement — creating an irresolvable conflict between the sweep AC and the NON-GOALS clause.
findings.location: What section (sweep grep definition) / AC 'the rule-based sweep grep returns zero hits' / tests/unit/test_completion_floor.py:261
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the sweep AC to: 'the rule-based sweep grep returns zero hits that still claim default-OFF/inert-by-default for `remediation_mode` or `novelty_drop_active` (or their floors), across src/ + tests/ + docs/ — hits about `completion_floor_active` are excluded (it remains False by NON-GOALS).' Alternatively, scope the grep to only match in proximity to the two flag names.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:324` ('By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert') to the plan's known-hits enumeration so executors are guided to update it alongside the other enumerated locations.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:324` contains the phrase 'By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert' (confirmed by search), which will be a stale-framing hit under the normative grep but is absent from the plan's known-hits enumeration for that file (only lines :81, :188-195, and ~523 are listed). An executor following the enumerated list will miss this residual.
findings.location: What section (known-hits list for src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py) / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:324
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the known-hits list under plan_review/__init__.py: `:324 ('By default the flag is False, so the floor is inert' — update to reference explicit False as the back-out)`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
